revision.comment: Auto generating page. date placed far in the past so it is attributed to Immanuelle and FireflyBot sends them messages if it is ever imported to Wikipedia.

revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=塩谷神社 |画像 |所在地=[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]]海老山町8-12 |祭神=[[猿田彦神]] [[塩土老翁神]] |社格 |創建=(伝)[[推古天皇]]26年 [[618年]] |本殿 |別名 |例祭=7月第2土曜日 |神事 |}} 塩谷神社（しおやじんじゃ）とは[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]]海老山町にある神社である。
==例祭== *茅の輪くぐり *例大祭 巫女清祓い *河原津神楽団奉納
==境内の他の神社== {{神社 |名称=龍宮神社 |画像 |所在地=[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]]海老山町8-12 |祭神= |社格 |創建 |本殿 |別名 |例祭=3月10日 |神事 |}} *龍宮神社 {{神社 |名称=湯蓋道空社 |画像 |所在地=[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]]海老山町8-12 |祭神=湯蓋道空 |社格 |創建= |本殿 |別名 |例祭=3月春分の日 |神事 |}} *湯蓋道空社 ==外部リンク== *[http://www.hachimansan.jp/sioya.html 五日市八幡神社 塩谷神社] *[http://shioyajinja.com/hoka.html 広島 塩谷神社]

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}. In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. V..."
revision.text:
'''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' (塩土老翁) is a {{ill|kami|ja|神 (神道)}} appearing in {{ill|Japanese mythology|ja|日本神話}}.
In the {{ill|Kojiki|ja|古事記}}, he is referred to as '''Shiotsuchi no Kami''' (塩椎神), while in the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}, he appears as '''Shiotsuchi no Oji''' or '''Shiotsu no Oji''' (塩土老翁/塩筒老翁). Additionally, the {{ill|Sendai Kuji Hongi|ja|先代旧事本紀}} also mentions him as Shiotsuchi no Oji. Variations of his name include "塩土老翁神" and "塩土翁神," with the character for "塩" sometimes written in its archaic form "鹽." Pronunciations include "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami" or "Shiotsuchi no Oji no Kami."
== Overview == The name "Shiotsuchi" can be interpreted as "Master of the Tides" or "Way of the Tides," associating the deity with control over tidal currents and navigation. In {{ill|Kiki mythology|ja|記紀神話}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji plays a critical role, offering guidance and information to key figures. His portrayal as a coastal deity who imparts wisdom has parallels to the "Old Man of the Sea" motif found in {{ill|Greek mythology|ja|ギリシア神話}}, such as {{ill|Proteus|ja|プローテウス}}. Additionally, he is revered as a god of salt production.
The shrine tradition of {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} in {{ill|Shiogama|ja|塩竈市}}, {{ill|Miyagi Prefecture|ja|宮城県}}, regards him as the deity who guided {{ill|Takemikazuchi|ja|タケミカヅチ}} and {{ill|Futsunushi|ja|フツヌシ}} during their pacification of the land. Shiotsuchi no Oji remained in the area to teach fishing and salt production to the people, making him a prominent deity in these domains.
== Legends == In the {{ill|Nihon Shoki|ja|日本書紀}}’s account of the {{ill|Descent of the Heavenly Grandson|ja|天孫降臨}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji is identified as {{ill|Kotosakukuni Katsunagasa|ja|事勝国勝長狭神}}. He offers his land to {{ill|Ninigi|ja|ニニギ}} upon his arrival at Kasasa Cape. Another tradition identifies him as a son of {{ill|Izanagi|ja|イザナギ}}.
In the myth of {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|山幸彦と海幸彦}}, Shiotsuchi no Oji assists {{ill|Hoori|ja|ホオリ}}, who had lost his brother's fishing hook. He provides a small boat (or tightly woven basket) and instructs Hoori to let the tides guide him to the palace of the sea deity {{ill|Watatsumi|ja|ワタツミ}}.
During the {{ill|Eastern Expedition of Emperor Jimmu|ja|神武東征}}, the Nihon Shoki recounts that Shiotsuchi no Oji inspired Emperor {{ill|Jimmu|ja|神武天皇}} to head east, stating, "There is good land to the east."
== Shrines == Shiotsuchi no Oji is enshrined at notable shrines, including: * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine|ja|鹽竈神社}} (Shiogama City, Miyagi Prefecture) – The main shrine. * {{ill|Shiogama Shrine (Tokyo Minato)|ja|鹽竈神社 (東京都港区)}} (Tokyo) * {{ill|Takamu Shrine|ja|高室神社}} (Kakegawa City, Shizuoka Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Okazaki)|ja|六所神社 (岡崎市)}} (Okazaki City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Rokusho Shrine (Toyota)|ja|六所神社 (豊田市)}} (Toyota City, Aichi Prefecture) * {{ill|Shioya Shrine|ja|塩屋神社}} (Hiroshima City, Hiroshima Prefecture)
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|ja|日本の神の一覧}} * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|ja|オオヤマツミ}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|ja|海幸山幸}} * {{ill|Ninigi|ja|ニニギ}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|ja|住吉三神}}

{{神社 |名称=塩屋神社 |画像=[[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）]] |所在地=[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]][[海老山町 (広島市)|海老山町]]8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 21 |緯度秒 = 46.2 |経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 21 |経度秒 = 53.7 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |祭神=[[猿田彦神]]、[[塩土老翁神]] |社格 |創建=（伝）[[推古天皇]]26年（[[618年]]） |本殿 |別名 |例祭=7月第2土曜日 |神事 }} '''塩屋神社'''（しおやじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]][[海老山町 (広島市)|海老山町]]にある神社である。 == 概要 == 境内には[[ケヤキ|欅]]の'''大慶の木'''がある。 そばにある立看板には{{Quote|枝が人差し指を合わせたようなOKという形に見えます。
願い事を叶えていただけそうです。|塩屋神社|塩屋神社境内}} == 歴史 == * [[推古天皇]]26年（[[618年]]）頃：塩屋神社が建立されたとされる。 * [[平成]]28年（[[2015年]]）：鎮座一千四百年記念事業として境内整備。 * 平成30年（[[2018年]]）：鎮座一千四百年。 == 例祭 == 七夕の行事と併せて行われる。 * 茅の輪くぐり * 例大祭 * 河原津神楽団奉納（令和7年現在奉納されていない） == 境内の他の神社 == * 龍宮神社 {{神社 |名称=龍宮神社 |画像 |所在地=塩屋神社と同じ |祭神=[[豊玉姫命]]、[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]] |社格 |創建=[[明治]]26年（[[1893年]]） |本殿 |別名 |例祭=[[旧暦]]：3月10日 |神事 |}} * 湯蓋道空社 {{神社 |名称=湯蓋道空社 |画像 |所在地=塩屋神社と同じ |祭神=湯蓋道空命 |社格 |創建= |本殿 |別名 |例祭=3月：[[春分の日]] |神事 |}} {{-}} == 関連項目 == * [[五日市八幡神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ 五日市八幡神社] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ 塩屋神社] {{デフォルトソート:しおやしんしや}} [[Category:広島市の神社]] [[Category:佐伯区の歴史]] [[Category:佐伯区の建築物]]

[[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima City]] [[Category:History of Saeki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Saeki Ward]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:塩屋神社]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|List [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == Related Topics == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima City]] [[Category:History of Saeki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Saeki Ward]] [[category:new pages]][[ja:塩屋神社]] == Related Topics == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en}}[[ja:日本の神の一覧]] * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|en}}[[ja:オオヤマツミ]] * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|en}}[[ja:海幸山幸]] * {{ill|Ninigi|en}}[[ja:ニニギ]] * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en}}[[ja:住吉三神]] [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == Related Topics == [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社}} * [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Old Lunar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社}} * [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Lunisolar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Old Calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima City]] [[Category:History of Saeki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |Image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |Image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社}} [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * [[ja:塩屋神社]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618年}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015年}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018年}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893rd year }}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社}} == External Links == * said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year }}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year }}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year }}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|12=draft}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year|12=draft}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year|12=draft}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|12=draft}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893rd year|12=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日|12=draft}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社|12=draft}} {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|12=draft|12=draft}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|12=draft|12=draft}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神|12=draft|12=draft}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|12=draft|12=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|12=draft|12=draft}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|12=draft|12=draft}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅|12=draft|12=draft}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|12=draft|12=draft}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|12=draft|12=draft}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year|12=draft|12=draft}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year|12=draft|12=draft}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|12=draft|12=draft}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|12=draft|12=draft}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893rd year|12=draft|12=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=draft|12=draft}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日|12=draft|12=draft}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|12=draft|12=draft}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en}} * {{ill|Oyamatsumi|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|en}} * {{ill|Ninigi|en|12=draft|12=draft}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en}} {{draft categories| |12=draft|12=draft}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima City]] [[Category:History of Saeki Ward]] [[Category:Buildings in Saeki Ward]] [[Category:Completion tier 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shiotsuchi no Oji]] [[Category:Japanese deities]] {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rituals= }} == revision.text:
{{神社 |名称=塩屋神社 |画像=[[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）]] |所在地=[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]][[海老山町 (広島市)|海老山町]]8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |緯度度 = 34 |緯度分 = 21 |緯度秒 = 46.2 |経度度 = 132 |経度分 = 21 |経度秒 = 53.7 |地図 = Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |祭神=[[猿田彦神]]、[[塩土老翁神]] |社格 |創建=（伝）[[推古天皇]]26年（[[618年]]） |本殿 |別名 |例祭=7月第2土曜日 |神事 }} '''塩屋神社'''（しおやじんじゃ）は、[[広島県]][[広島市]][[佐伯区]][[海老山町 (広島市)|海老山町]]にある神社である。 == 概要 == 境内には[[ケヤキ|欅]]の'''大慶の木'''がある。 そばにある立看板には{{Quote|枝が人差し指を合わせたようなOKという形に見えます。
願い事を叶えていただけそうです。|塩屋神社|塩屋神社境内}} == 歴史 == * [[推古天皇]]26年（[[618年]]）頃：塩屋神社が建立されたとされる。 * [[平成]]28年（[[2015年]]）：鎮座一千四百年記念事業として境内整備。 * 平成30年（[[2018年]]）：鎮座一千四百年。 == 例祭 == 七夕の行事と併せて行われる。 * 茅の輪くぐり * 例大祭 * 河原津神楽団奉納（令和7年現在奉納されていない） == 境内の他の神社 == * 龍宮神社 {{神社 |名称=龍宮神社 |画像 |所在地=塩屋神社と同じ |祭神=[[豊玉姫命]]、[[タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|玉依姫命]] |社格 |創建=[[明治]]26年（[[1893年]]） |本殿 |別名 |例祭=[[旧暦]]：3月10日 |神事 |}} * 湯蓋道空社 {{神社 |名称=湯蓋道空社 |画像 |所在地=塩屋神社と同じ |祭神=湯蓋道空命 |社格 |創建= |本殿 |別名 |例祭=3月：[[春分の日]] |神事 |}} {{-}} == 関連項目 == * [[五日市八幡神社]] == 外部リンク == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ 五日市八幡神社] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ 塩屋神社] {{デフォルトソート:しおやしんしや}} [[Category:広島市の神社]] [[Category:佐伯区の歴史]] [[Category:佐伯区の建築物]]

{{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅|qq=draft|qq=draft}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Shioya shrine.JPG|qq=}} |Location={{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-kami|qq=}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiodotaro Ojin|qq=}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} 26 ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/618 AD|qq=}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zelkova|qq=}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/618 AD|qq=}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015 AD|qq=}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018 AD|qq=}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|lt=Tamayori-hime|lt=Gyokui-hime|lt_ja=玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayoribime|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|qq=}} |Rank= |Founded={{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji|13=User:Immanuelle/Meiji era|qq=}} 26 ({{ill|1893 AD|ja|1893rd year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/1893 AD|qq=}}) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: {{ill|Vernal Equinox Day|ja|春分の日|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Vernal Equinox Day|qq=}} |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Spring Equinox (March) |Rituals= }} {{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=}} == External Links == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ Shioya Shrine] {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shioya Shrine (Karatsu)]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima City]] [[Category:History of Saeki-ku]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Saeki-ku]] }} [[sn:Shioya Shrine (Karatsu)]] [[ja:塩屋神社]] [[Category:Merged pages]]

Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|ja|広島県|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima Prefecture|qq=}} {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}} {{ill|Saeki Ward|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki Ward|qq=}} {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova|ja|ケヤキ|lt=Zelkova|lt_ja=欅|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Zelkova|qq=}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|ja|推古天皇|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress Suiko|qq=}} ({{ill|618 AD|ja|618th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/618 AD|qq=}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heisei|qq=}} 28 ({{ill|2015 AD|ja|2015th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2015 AD|qq=}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018 AD|ja|2018th year|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/2018 AD|qq=}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=}}、{{ill|Tamayoribime|ja|タマヨリビメ (日向神話)|1=Tamayori-hime (mother of {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinto shrine|qq=}} located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|ja|佐伯区|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|qq=}}, {{ill|Hiroshima City|ja|広島市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiroshima City|qq=}}, [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=}}, Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image=
