|AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Shioya shrine.JPG|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiodotaro Ojin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: [[:en:Vernal Equinox Day|Vernal Equinox Day]] |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|lt=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Oyamatsumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|qq=}} * {{ill|Umisachi and Yamasachi|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Umisachi and Yamasachi|qq=}} * {{ill|Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi-no-Mikoto|lt=Ninigi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ninigi|qq=}} * {{ill|Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi sanjin|lt=Sumiyoshi Sanjin|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sumiyoshi Sanjin|qq=}} {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shrines in Hiroshima City]] religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Spring Equinox (March) |Rituals= }} {{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ Shioya Shrine] {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Hiroshima City]] }} [[sn:Shioya Shrine (Karatsu)]] [[ja:塩屋神社]] [[sn:Shioya Shrine (Hiroshima)]] [[Category:佐伯区の建築物]] [[Category:佐伯区の歴史]] [[Category:Shrines in {{Shrine |Name=Shioya Shrine |image={{ill|File:Shioya shrine.JPG|ja|塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|lt=Shioya Shrine (with Ryugu Shrine in the far rear and the Daikei Tree on the left)|lt_ja=塩屋神社（右奥が龍宮神社、左の木が大慶の木）|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/File:Shioya shrine.JPG|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} |Location=[[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO=JP-34 |LatitudeDeg=34 |LatitudeMin=21 |LatitudeSec=46.2 |LongitudeDeg=132 |LongitudeMin=21 |LongitudeSec=53.7 |Map=Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |Deity={{ill|Sarutahiko-no-kami|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-kami|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}、{{ill|Shiodotaro Ojin|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiodotaro Ojin|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |Rank= |Founded=(trad.) [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: [[:en:Vernal Equinox Day|Vernal Equinox Day]] |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * invalid}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ Shioya Shrine] {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: [[:en:Vernal Equinox Day|Vernal Equinox Day]] |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of [[sn:hioya Shrine (Hiroshima)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Spring Equinox (March) |Rituals= }} {{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ Shioya Shrine] {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: [[:en:Vernal Equinox Day|Vernal Equinox Day]] |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * invalid}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of [[sn:hioya Shrine (Hiroshima)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Spring Equinox (March) |Rituals= }} {{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ Itsukaichi [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: [[:en:Vernal Equinox Day|Vernal Equinox Day]] |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of [[sn:hioya Shrine (Hiroshima)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Spring Equinox (March) |Rituals= }} {{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ Shioya Shrine] {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |image = File:Shioya shrine.JPG }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lunisolar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} * Yugai Dokusa Shrine {{Shrine |Name=Yugai Dokusa Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity=Yugai Dokusa-no-mikoto |Rank= |Founded= |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=March: [[:en:Vernal Equinox Day|Vernal Equinox Day]] |Rituals= }} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * {{ill|https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|ja|五日市八幡神社|lt=Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|lt_ja=五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} * {{ill|https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|ja|塩屋神社|lt=Shioya Shrine|lt_ja=塩屋神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/|qq=}} == See Also == * {{ill|List of Japanese deities|en|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of Japanese deities|qq=}} * {{ill|Ōyamatsumi|lt=Ōyamatsumi|lt=Oyamatsumi|en|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oyamatsumi|qq=}} * [[sn:hioya Shrine (Hiroshima)]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= Yugatamichi-no-Mikoto (湯蓋道空命) |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Spring Equinox (March) |Rituals= }} {{-}} == See Also == * {{ill|Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|ja|五日市八幡神社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} == External Links == * [https://www.hachiman-jinja.jp/ Itsukaichi Hachiman Shrine] * [https://www.shioya-jinja.jp/ Shioya Shrine] {{translated page|ja|塩屋神社|version=103635435|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Completion tier 4]] }} [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Saeki-ku|qid=Q73718}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.362833333333334|N|132.36491666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}} }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Saeki-ku|qid=Q73718}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.362833333333334|N|132.36491666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}} }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ}}、[[:en:Tamayori-hime (mother of Jimmu)|Gyokui-hime]] |Rank= |Founded=[[:en:Meiji era|Meiji era]] 26 ([[:en:1893|1893 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival={{ill|Lunisolar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Saeki-ku|qid=Q73718}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.362833333333334|N|132.36491666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}} }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q42633835}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine |Deity={{ill|Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotamahime-no-mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] 26 ([[:en:618|618 AD]]) |Main Hall= |Alternate Name= |Annual Festival=Second Saturday of July |Rituals= |prefecture={{ill|Saeki-ku|qid=Q73718}} |coordinates={{Coord|34.362833333333334|N|132.36491666666666|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |deity={{ill|Sarutahiko Ōkami|qid=Q3090037}} }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|Hiroshima Prefecture]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]] [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki Ward]] {{ill|Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|ja|海老山町 (広島市)|lt=Ebiyama Town|lt_ja=海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town (Hiroshima)|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q42633835}}. == Overview == Within the shrine grounds stands the '''Daikei Tree''' of [[:en:Zelkova serrata|Zelkova]]. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name=Ryugu Shrine |image= |Location=Same as Shioya Shrine Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|qq=|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine
