[[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to [[Category:pages with merged translations]] ==Merged second translation== {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Shioya Shrine (塩屋神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= [[File:Shioya shrine.JPG|250px|Shioya Shrine (Ryugu Shrine in the background, Daikei Tree on the left)]] |location= [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of [[:en:Empress Suiko|Empress Suiko]] ([[:en:618|618 AD]]): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In [[:en:Heisei era|Heisei]] 28 ([[:en:2015|2015 AD]]): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ([[:en:2018|2018 AD]]): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious building| title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]], {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to トヨタマヒメ|1=Toyotama-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2329261}}, {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Old Japanese calendar|13=User:Immanuelle/Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a [[:en:Shinto shrine|Shinto shrine]] located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, [[:en:Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima]], [[:en:Hiroshima|Hiroshima City]], [[:en:Hiroshima Prefecture|広島県]]. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * [[:en:Empress Suiko|Suiko]] 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Toyotama-hime|ja|豊玉姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Toyotama-hime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese Calendar|qq=}}: March 10 |Rituals= }} === Yugatamichi Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Yugatamichi Shrine (湯蓋道空社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|en|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q73718|lt=Saeki-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|WD=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima City}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=広島県}}. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine the '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|618|en|618|WD=Q31035|lt=618 AD}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei}} 28 ({{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015 AD}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018 AD}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|comment=changed title based on Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=広島県}}. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|WD=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova|1=Zelkova serrata}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|618|en|618|WD=Q31035|lt=618 AD|1=618}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 28 ({{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015 AD|1=2015}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018 AD|1=2018}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}} 8-12 |ISO = JP-34 |Latitude = 34 | LatMinutes = 21 | LatSeconds = 46.2 |Longitude = 132 | LongMinutes = 21 | LongSeconds = 53.7 |Map= Japan Hiroshima city Urban area |deity= {{ill|Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja|猿田彦神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sarutahiko-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to サルタヒコ|1=Sarutahiko Ōkami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3090037}}, {{ill|Shiotsuchioji|ja|塩土老翁神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shiotsuchioji|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to シオツチオジ|1=Shiotsuchi-no-Oji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11174872}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= Traditional date: 618 CE (Suiko 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= Second Saturday of July |Rituals= }} '''Shioya Shrine''' (塩屋神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Q20038243|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|en|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|WD=Q73718|lt=Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|1=Saeki-ku, Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|WD=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima City|1=Hiroshima}}, {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=広島県|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |name= Ryugu Shrine (龍宮神社) | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image= |location= Same as Shioya Shrine |deity= {{ill|Tamayorihime|ja|玉依姫命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamayorihime|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to タマヨリビメ|1=Tamayori-hime|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q56344982}} |ShrineRank= |consecration_year= 1893 CE (Meiji 26) |MainHall= |Alternatename= |AnnualFestival= {{ill|Old Japanese calendar|ja|旧暦|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|1=Japanese Lunisolar Calendar|11=Japanese Lunisolar It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building| (塩屋神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Hiroshima Prefecture|en|Hiroshima Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=Hiroshima Prefecture|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}} {{ill|Hiroshima|en|Hiroshima|WD=Q34664|lt=Hiroshima '''Daikei Tree''' of {{ill|Zelkova serrata|en|Zelkova serrata|WD=Q920344|lt=Zelkova|1=Zelkova serrata}}. Beside it, an upright sign bears the inscription: {{Quote|The branches resemble an OK sign formed as if an index finger were raised.
It appears that wishes may be granted.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * Around the 26th year of {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Empress Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} ({{ill|618|en|618|WD=Q31035|lt=618 AD|1=618}}): Shioya Shrine is said to have been established. * In {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 28 ({{ill|2015|en|2015|WD=Q2002|lt=2015 AD|1=2015}}): Renovations were carried out on the grounds as part of the 1400th-anniversary commemoration of its enshrinement. * In Heisei 30 ({{ill|2018|en|2018|WD=Q25291|lt=2018 AD|1=2018}}): The 1400th anniversary of the shrine’s enshrinement was observed. == Annual Festival == Held in conjunction with the Tanabata festival. * Passing through the straw ring * Grand Festival * Dedication of the Kawahara Tsukagura Group (Not currently dedicated as of Reiwa 7) == Other Shrines on the Grounds == * Ryugu Shrine {{Infobox religious A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival Hiroshima|ja|海老山町|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebiyama Town, Hiroshima|1=Q20038243|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q20038243}}, {{ill|Saeki-ku, Hiroshima|en|Saeki-ku, Prefecture|WD=Q617375|lt=広島県|1=Hiroshima Prefecture}}. == Overview == The shrine grounds feature a large [[zelkova tree]] known as the '''Daikei Tree'''. A sign on the premises reads: {{Quote|The branches resemble the shape of "OK" with fingers pointing upwards. It seems like a place where your wishes may come true.|Shioya Shrine|Shioya Shrine grounds}} == History == * {{ill|Empress Suiko|en|Empress Suiko|WD=Q298057|lt=Suiko|1=Empress Suiko}} 26 (618 CE): Traditionally believed to be the year of the shrine's establishment. * [[:en:Heisei period|Heisei]] 28 (2015 CE): Grounds renovation as part of the 1,400th anniversary commemoration. * Heisei 30 (2018 CE): 1,400th anniversary of the shrine's foundation. == festival == The shrine festival coincides with Tanabata traditions, including: * Chinowa Kuguri (Passing through the grass ring) * Main Festival and Shrine Maiden’s Purification Rites * Kaguradan dedication by the Kawarazu Kagura Troupe == Other Shrines within the Grounds == === Ryugu Shrine === {{Infobox religious building|
